title: Milla nivalis
taxonomy: Milla nivalis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asphodelaceae, which is a family of monocotyledonous plants commonly known as asphodels. Milla nivalis is a member of the tribe Asphodelodeae. This plant is native to the mountainous regions of Central and South America. It is a perennial herb with white or pink flowers that bloom in the summer months. Milla nivalis is often found growing in alpine meadows and rocky outcroppings at high elevations.
uses: Milla nivalis, a plant native to the alpine regions of Europe, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Its rhizomes and roots are used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and wounds.
